channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ¿Reducir la inflamación podría tratar la depresión?
item.description: Alrededor del 25 por ciento de las personas con depresión presentan niveles elevados de proteínas inflamatorias en la sangre.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith

item.title: Los celulares parecen haber reducido la natalidad. Pero no nos engañemos
item.description: La crisis de fertilidad parece ser una crisis de conexión.
item.dc:creator: Christine Emba

media:content.media:credit: Margaret Murphy para The New York Times

item.title: Las vacaciones literarias están de moda
item.description: Los clubes de lectura en sitios turísticos, las agencias de viajes, las bibliotecas de los hoteles y un número cada vez mayor de festivales ofrecen a los lectores nuevas formas de dar rienda suelta a sus intereses.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Glusac

item.title: Un sacerdote griego y su drone metal irrumpen en la escena musical
item.description: Dionysios Tabakis nunca se ha aventurado más allá de Turquía, pero este verano debutará en el escenario de un famoso festival en Filadelfia.
item.dc:creator: Hester Underhill

media:content.media:credit: Myrto Papadopoulos para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Dionysios Tabakis es sacerdote en Nafplio, Grecia, donde ha servido a la misma congregación durante casi 30 años.

item.title: What if You Root for the Other N.B.A. Team in New York?
item.description: The Knicks championship run has elated fans, but it has been a tough few weeks for supporters of the Nets. “I feel sick,” one Nets die-hard said.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní

media:content.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Knicks fans have been ecstatic since their team’s long-awaited N.B.A. championship.

item.title: Estoy enamorado de mi doctor
item.description: Pero tenemos una gran diferencia de edad.
item.dc:creator: W. Blake Martin

item.title: Democratic Socialists Took City Hall. Now They’re Aiming at Congress.
item.description: After propelling Zohran Mamdani to become mayor of New York City, the Democratic Socialists of America are trying to take down incumbents in the House and the State Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays and Benjamin Oreskes

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart, Victor J. Blue, & Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Claire Valdez, Conrad Blackburn and Darializa Avila Chevalier, from left to right, are being backed by the Democratic Socialists of America in New York City.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 20, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: World Cup 2026: See the U.S. Goal That Was Allowed to Stand After Video Review
item.description: View in 3-D how the U.S. men’s soccer team scored on a set piece against Australia in a 2-0 win.
item.dc:creator: Bora Erden, Malika Khurana, Allison McCann, Karthik Patanjali, Bedel Saget, Raj Saha and Jeremy White
item.category: United States Men's National Soccer Team

item.title: El dolor de cuidar a un padre que te maltrató
item.description: Millones de adultos en EE. UU. cuidan a padres que abusaron de ellos en la infancia, atrapados entre la culpa, la obligación y un sistema de salud que no ofrece alternativas.
item.dc:creator: Katie Engelhart

media:content.media:credit: Preston Gannaway para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Carole con su padre, Andre, el 29 de mayo

item.title: L.A.P.D. Releases Bodycam Footage of Officer Shooting a Knicks Fan’s Dog
item.description: The killing of the dog last Saturday sparked an international outcry and prompted questions about the use of force by police officers.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Photos of Jameson, center, a dog shot by a Los Angeles Police Department officer. The incident has caused international outcry.

item.title: Cape Verde Will Count on Vozinha Against Uruguay at the World Cup
item.description: Cape Verde’s veteran goalkeeper, Vozinha, is keeping the archipelago nation’s hopes outsized after his performance against soccer giant Spain.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean, Andre Amaral and Saikou Jammeh

media:content.media:description: Cape Verde’s veteran goalkeeper, Josimar José Évora Dias, after the team played to a scoreless tie with Spain on Monday.

item.title: Quote of the Day: How Tiny Curaçao Vaulted Itself Into the World Cup
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, June 20, 2026

item.title: A 175-Year-Old Shipwreck, Lots of Booze and a Korean Island’s Quest to Survive
item.description: A South Korean island is reviving a long‑forgotten episode involving a French whaler as a wine festival, in an effort to stay relevant.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun and Woohae Cho

item.title: How These High Schoolers Are Preparing for the 2026 Jimmy Awards
item.description: Each June, students from around the country come to New York for the Jimmy Awards. We tagged along for a day of their intensive musical-theater residency.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli and Dolly Faibyshev

media:content.media:credit: Dolly Faibyshev for The New York Times

item.title: Charles Kushner, U.S. Ambassador to France, Riles Paris With Trump Diplomacy
item.description: The U.S. envoy to Paris has emerged as the embodiment of combative diplomacy in the age of President Trump, riling the French establishment notably with his accusations of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler

media:content.media:description: Charles Kushner, the U.S. ambassador to France, greeting President Trump on Monday at the Group of 7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France.

item.title: Rubio aprobó la detención de un migrante que criticó a De la Espriella
item.description: En el documento, el secretario de Estado indicaba que las críticas de Beto Coral hacia un candidato presidencial de su natal Colombia, socavaban la política exterior de EE. UU.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz and Annie Correal

item.title: ‘Get Outta Here!’
item.description: Katie Hoody keeps the hits coming.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: There’s more than meets the eye to many of the clues in this solve.

item.title: Memo by Rubio Approved Detention of Immigrant Who Criticized Trump Ally
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Beto Coral’s criticism of a presidential candidate in his home country of Colombia undermined U.S. foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz and Annie Correal

item.title: Seattle Is Now a World Cup Draw: ‘Just a Great Soccer City’
item.description: Seattle has become a leading American soccer city with successful professional franchises and a devoted fan base.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin and Ruth Fremson

media:content.media:description: Lumen Field has become a major U.S. soccer venue as the home of the Seattle Sounders of M.L.S. and the Seattle Reign of the N.W.S.L.

item.title: ‘A Literal Coverup’: What Is the Kennedy Center Hiding Behind Those Tarps?
item.description: The arts center says it pulled President Trump’s name from the building front. But the result remains hidden by tarps, prompting questions and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Williamson
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:description: Massive tarps hanging over the front of the Kennedy Center on Thursday.

item.title: Amazon’s Movie Arm Abandons Film About OpenAI
item.description: The company, which invested $50 billion in the artificial intelligence start-up this year, will let the team behind the film, “Artificial,” try to sell the project to another studio.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Sperling

media:content.media:description: Sam Altman, the chief executive of OpenAI, is the focus of the film.

item.title: A New Air Force One, via Qatar, Nears Its First Presidential Flight
item.description: President Trump showed off the new red, white and blue Boeing 747, a gift from Qatar that prompted a storm of criticism from lawmakers, before a flyover on July 4.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs

media:content.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump after a tour of the new Air Force One aircraft at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland on Friday. He called it “a flying White House at a level of luxury that nobody’s ever seen before.”

item.title: The Reflecting Pool Appears to Be Rejecting Its Makeover
item.description: As National Park Service crews try to clean the algae that turned the water bright green, another problem has developed: The “American flag blue” coating is coming off.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi

media:content.media:description: A National Park Service crew cleaning algae from the Reflecting Pool on Friday.

item.title: Washington Reenactors
item.description: Washington Reenactors

media:content.media:credit: Martin Schoeller for The New York Times

item.title: James Bradley, Co-Author of ‘Flags of Our Fathers,’ Dies at 72
item.description: His best-selling book celebrated the servicemen in the stirring photograph of the U.S. flag-raising on Iwo Jima. One, it was long believed, was his father.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

media:content.media:description: James Bradley in 2006. His father, a Navy medic, spoke little of his time in combat, and Mr. Bradley sought to highlight his valor, including actions that earned him the Navy Cross.

item.title: Moscow Tanker Blast Most Likely Russian Missile, Video Shows
item.description: A dramatic explosion that caused the lid of an oil tanker to fly into the sky during a Ukrainian aerial assault on Moscow was most likely caused by a Russian air defense missile, verified video shows.
item.dc:creator: James McManagan, Paul Sonne, Malachy Browne and Jackeline Luna

item.title: Two Trains Collide Near Bedford, England, on Friday
item.description: At least one person was killed and a “number of people” were injured after two passenger trains crashed in south-central England on Friday, the authorities said.

media:content.media:credit: Jordan Reynolds/Press Association, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Police at Progress Park in Bedford, England, after two passenger trains collided on Friday.

item.title: California Sheriff’s Deputy Is Charged in Fatal On-Duty Crash
item.description: The deputy was responding to a call, driving at about 100 miles per hour, when he struck a Tesla, killing its driver and severely injuring his fiancée, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Diaz

item.title: Trump Shifts Stance in South Carolina After Losses in Governor’s Races
item.description: On Friday the president made the unusual move of endorsing both candidates in the Republican runoff for South Carolina governor, after initially supporting one of them.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina and Bayliss Wagner

media:content.media:description: President Trump first endorsed Pamela Evette, South Carolina’s lieutenant governor, in late May.

item.title: Scottish Soccer Invasion in Boston for the World Cup
item.description: Thousands of Scottish soccer fans descended upon Boston to watch their team play in the World Cup for the first time in nearly 30 years. Scotland beat Haiti 1-0 in the first round at Gillette Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: Thousands of soccer fans from Scotland descended on Boston to see their team play in the World Cup.

item.title: Ruling in Missouri Restores Access to Medication Abortions
item.description: The court found that dozens of state laws undermined a state constitutional amendment that guaranteed reproductive rights. The state vowed to appeal the finding.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Hubler
item.category: Dobbs v Jackson Women's Health Organization (Supreme Court Decision)

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Medication abortions will now be accessible in Missouri, following a two-year court battle between the state and Planned Parenthood.

item.title: Lebanon Emerges as Weak Link in U.S.-Iran Deal to End War
item.description: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, once seen as a secondary front to the American-Israeli war on Iran, has become one of the main obstacles to ending it.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Outside the grave of the former Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah, who was killed in an Israeli strike, in Beirut on Tuesday.

item.title: Trains Collide in UK, Killing at Least One and Injuring Dozens of Others
item.description: At least one person was killed and dozens of others were injured in the crash near Bedford, England, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar

media:content.media:description: Emergency service workers at the scene of a collision involving two trains near Bedford, England, on Friday.

item.title: Israel Is Likely to Continue Attacks in Lebanon, U.S. Intelligence Concludes
item.description: The new cease-fire between the United States and Iran calls for an end to fighting in Lebanon, but U.S. intelligence officials do not expect Israel to halt its strikes on Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: A man searching for his belongings after an Israeli strike on Tyre, Lebanon, on Friday.

item.title: Overlooked No More: Robbie Basho, Guitar Mystic Who Sought Enlightenment in Sound
item.description: He lived with chronic pain and deep loneliness, which he poured into boundary-pushing music that incorporated multicultural sounds.
item.dc:creator: Howard Fishman

item.title: James Burrows, Master of the TV Sitcom, Dies at 85
item.description: Beloved by actors, he helped create “Cheers” and directed more than 1,000 episodes of hit shows like “Taxi,” “Friends” and “The Big Bang Theory.”
item.dc:creator: Glenn Rifkin
item.category: The Mary Tyler Moore Show (TV Program)

media:content.media:credit: Alex Welsh for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: James Burrows in 2023. He won 11 Emmy Awards and received 47 nominations in a career that spanned five decades.

item.title: Californians Get Stingier at the Ballot Box as Costs Rise
item.description: Voters rejected more local taxes than usual in the June election, a sign that they are feeling the pinch from rising costs.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla

media:content.media:credit: Minh Connors for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kevin Stull, right, fills out a ballot as Bryan Diaz, a barber, waits to give a haircut at Ana’s Hair Salon on Election Day in San Francisco.

item.title: Lebanon Cease-Fire Bolsters U.S.-Iran Truce, After a Shaky Start
item.description: Talks on a long-term peace deal were set to begin on Friday, but Iran pulled out, citing Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Hours later, diplomats say, Israel and Hezbollah agreed to halt fighting there.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Euan Ward, Adam Rasgon, Johnatan Reiss and Richard Pérez-Peña
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Cleaning up after Israeli strikes in Touline, southern Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: What to Know About the Storm That Brought Deadly Flooding to the South
item.description: An early-season storm that in some places dumped around two feet of rain also killed three people. Louisiana and Mississippi were hit hard.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen and Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: David Grunfeld/The Times-Picayune/The New Orleans Advocate, Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Flooding from Tropical Storm Arthur in New Orleans on Thursday.

item.title: What Could Taylor Swift’s Bachelorette Party Look Like?
item.description: Recent photos of guests arriving at the singer’s seaside home have fueled online speculation that a pre-wedding celebration may be underway. Wedding experts weigh in on what such a fete might entail.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lyon

media:content.media:description: Swift’s house guests are fueling speculation that the singer may be hosting a pre-wedding celebration ahead of her anticipated nuptials to Travis Kelce.

item.title: Sebastian Vettel: Finding Calm While Driving a Racecar
item.description: As a Formula 1 racer, I could disappear and forget about time. Now, my greatest joy is discovering what puts me in the present.
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Vettel

item.title: Woman Receives 12-Year Sentence for 1978 Scalding Death of Stepdaughter, 5
item.description: Janice Nix was found guilty of manslaughter after her stepson told the police that she had forced his sister into a hot bath as a form of punishment in their home in South London.
item.dc:creator: Ali Watkins

item.title: U.S. to End AIDS Funding for South Africa
item.description: According to an unsigned statement from the State Department, the U.S. will phase out support for H.I.V. prevention and treatment in South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Zimasa Matiwane
item.category: President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief

media:content.media:description: A men’s health clinic in Johannesburg, South Africa, that closed last year after President Trump’s executive order slashed foreign aid.

item.title: ¿Quién es Andy Burnham, el que podría ser el próximo primer ministro del Reino Unido?
item.description: Carismático y de un optimismo desenfadado, Burnham es todo lo contrario a Keir Starmer. Sus aliados esperan que pueda mejorar la relación del Partido Laborista con los votantes.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:description: Andy Burnham, el político más popular del Partido Laborista, llegando el viernes al anuncio de los resultados de las elecciones parciales de Makerfield, en las que venció al partido populista de derecha Reform UK.

item.title: How Cubans Are Living in the Dark
item.description: Lisette Poole González, a photographer for The New York Times, talks with Jack Nicas, The Times’s Mexico City bureau chief, about photographing Havana at night amid an energy crisis.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas, Lisette Poole González, Leila Medina, Christina Thornell, Coleman Lowndes, Joey Sendaydiego and Thomas Vollkommer

item.title: Chanting and Celebrating, U.S. Soccer Fans March to the Match
item.description: Adopting a tradition from Europe, thousands of caffeinated and beer-fueled fans marched through Seattle to the contest between the United States and Australia.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin and Ruth Fremson

media:content.media:description: U.S. soccer fans march through Seattle to the match with Australia.

item.title: Giorgia Meloni le dice a Trump: ‘Italia y yo nunca rogamos’
item.description: El presidente Trump dijo que la primera ministra italiana le ‘rogó’ tomarse una foto juntos en la cumbre del G7 en Francia. El comentario ha desatado críticas de todo el gobierno italiano.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich and Josephine de La Bruyère
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump con la primera ministra italiana Giorgia Meloni tras una foto de grupo del G7 en Evian-les-Bains, Francia, el martes.

item.title: Why Aren’t There Big Vocalists Anymore?
item.description: The “Popcast” hosts, Joe Coscarelli and Jon Caramanica, answer listener questions, including, “Is Adele responsible for the extinction of big vocalists?”
item.dc:creator: Popcast

item.title: Catherine, Princess of Wales Questions Digital Screens’ Role in Children’s Lives
item.description: The princess published an essay Friday warning against a childhood “mediated by screens,” days after Britain announced a ban on social media for children under 16.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel

media:content.media:description: Catherine, Princess of Wales, visiting a municipal preschool during the first day of her visit to Reggio Emilia in May in Reggio Emilia, Italy.
